description: In an apartment living room, Joey stands with his arm around Chandler's shoulders. Ross stands to their left, and Monica stands behind them. Joey says, "That's great."
Joey keeps his arm around Chandler's shoulders.
Joey pats Chandler's chest and says, "You and my sister sitting in a tree."
Chandler looks at Joey.
Chandler smiles with his lips pressed together and says, "Yep, I'm in a tree."
The setting changes to an exterior shot of a lit apartment building at night. A car drives past.
Inside an apartment, Rachel walks through the door holding her coat and asks, "Did he call?"
Rachel closes the door behind her.
Rachel asks, "Did Mark call?" Monica, seated at a table writing, answers, "No, honey, I'm sorry. But the weekend's not over yet."
Rachel lowers her head and rests her hands, still holding the coat, against her stomach.
Rachel looks toward the ceiling and says, "Oh, my God. Is that Phoebe?"
Monica says, "Guess they're back from their date." A rhythmic squeaking sound begins. Rachel opens a window and says, "Ah, it's music."
Rachel reaches into a kitchen cabinet and grabs a glass. The rhythmic squeaking sound continues.
Rachel holds the glass and says, "Very nice."
Rachel turns back toward the room, her mouth slightly open. Monica looks up from her writing. The rhythmic squeaking sound continues.
Rachel's eyes widen and her mouth opens further. The rhythmic squeaking sound continues.
Rachel covers her mouth with her hand and says, "Oh, my God."
Rachel keeps her hand over her mouth, her eyes wide.
Rachel turns toward Monica and asks, "So how are you?" Monica answers, "I am good. I finished my book."
Rachel asks, "Oh, yeah? What's it about?" Monica says, "I don't remember. Do you want to"
Monica continues, "take a walk?" Rachel replies, "Yeah, I do." Both grab their coats.
Rachel and Monica quickly walk out the door. The setting changes to a different apartment where Ross sits on a stool holding a piece of paper and Chandler leans on a counter.
Ross looks at the paper and reads, "Dear Mary Angela."
Ross continues reading, "Hi. How's it going?"
Ross reads, "This is the hardest letter I've ever had to write," then lowers the paper and asks, "What the hell's the matter with you?"
Chandler looks up at Ross.
Ross points at Chandler and asks, "How do you think Joey's gonna react when he finds out you blew off his sister in a letter?"
Chandler steps forward, gesturing with one hand, and says, "Well, that's the part where you tell him that I moved to France"
Ross stares at Chandler.
Chandler continues, "when actually I'll be in"
Chandler stands straight, looking at Ross.
Ross hands the paper to Chandler and says, "look, look, you've got to do this yourself, okay? In person."
Ross points his finger and adds, "At least you know her name. You just go to the house and you ask for Mary"
Ross holds up his hand and continues, "Angela. Okay? When whichever one she is comes to the door you take her for a walk."
Ross says, "You let her down easy."
Chandler asks, "Okay. What if Mary Angela comes to the door and I ask for Mary Angela?"
Ross drinks from a red plastic cup.
Chandler points his thumb upward and says, "We're in Cuba."
The setting changes to an exterior shot of a city skyline and a bridge illuminated at night.
Chandler stands on a porch in front of a door. He knocks on the door, then rests his forehead against the door.
Chandler knocks again. The door opens and Joey stands inside.
Chandler says, "Joey,"
Joey looks at Chandler and opens the door wider.
Joey asks, "what are you doing here?" Joey answers himself, "Waiting for my grandma to finish my laundry. What about you?"
Chandler looks at Joey.
Chandler says, "I'm here to see Mary Angela." Joey smiles and says, "You are so the."
Joey puts his arm around Chandler and pulls him inside, closing the door behind them.
Joey turns to Chandler and says, "Now, look, listen,"
Joey steps closer to Chandler.
Joey gestures with his hands and says, "you gotta be cool because my grandma doesn't know about you two yet."
Joey continues, "And you do not want to tick her off. She was, like, the"
Joey finishes, "6th person to spit on Mussolini's hanging body."
Chandler stares at Joey with his mouth slightly open.
Chandler asks, "Where are your parents?"
Joey replies, "Oh, they're in Vermont buying cigarettes."
Chandler glances past Joey.
Chandler asks, "Where's Mary Angela?"
Joey turns his head toward the doorway.
Joey points his thumb over his shoulder and says, "She's right in there."
Chandler steps into a doorway. Several women sitting at a dining table, all with dark curly hair and wearing red clothing, turn toward him. The women speak in unison.
Joey smiles widely. Chandler smiles with his lips pressed together. The scene changes to a coffee shop where Ross sits at a table near Monica and Rachel on a couch.
Rachel says, "Can't believe Mark didn't call."
Rachel rests her head back on the couch and adds, "It's Sunday night and he didn't call. Bummer."
Ross looks at Rachel.
Ross flips a page of a magazine and says, "Yeah, right. Look at you."
Ross looks at Rachel and says, "You're practically giddy."
Ross says, "No, I'm genuinely sorry the Mark thing didn't work out."
Ross leans forward toward Rachel and says, "Look, Rach, I want only good things for you."
Monica leans forward and asks, "Wait a minute. Why don't you just call Mark?"
Ross rests his head near Rachel's shoulder.
Monica says, "I mean, who says you have to sit here and wait for him? You've got to make stuff happen."
Rachel replies, "But you don't want to seem too pushy."
Monica gestures with her hand and says, "Honey, it's not pushy. He gave you his home number."
Ross sits up quickly and asks, "He gave you his home number? As in, like, to his home?"
Rachel nods and says, "Yeah. And you don't mind if I call because you only want good things for me?"
Ross replies, "That's right. Good things. That is what I said."
Rachel looks at a portable phone in her hand. Ross stares straight ahead, his eyes wide.
In a coffee shop, Rachel stands near the counter holding a cordless phone to her ear. Ross and Monica stand nearby.
Rachel says, "Hello?" Ross looks at her.
Rachel says, "Mark? Hi, it's Rachel Green." She presses a finger to her other ear.
Rachel listens to the phone, looking down, then gasps slightly and looks up.
Rachel smiles and says, "Oh, no, don't you apologize." Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel nods as Ross watches her.
Rachel says, "Yeah, I'll hold." She covers the phone's mouthpiece and says, "He left my number at work."
Rachel continues, "He was going to get it, but he was helping his niece with her report on the pioneers." Ross tilts his head and presses his lips together.
Ross turns to Monica. Audience laughter is audible.
Ross looks at Monica and says, "So made up."
Rachel uncovers the phone's mouthpiece.
Rachel says into the phone, "Yeah. Oh, my God. Tomorrow?"
She writes on a notepad on the counter and says, "No, it's perfect."
Rachel smiles while writing and says, "Oh, God, thank you so much."
Rachel nods.
Rachel says, "Great. Bye."
Rachel lowers the phone, turns to Ross and Monica, and gasps.
She says, "I got the interview."
Monica steps forward and says, "Oh, yay. There you go." Rachel gestures with her hand and says, "He even offered to meet me for lunch and prep me for it."
Monica smiles and says, "Oh, that is a meeting." Ross nods and says, "Yeah. Well,"
Ross continues, "if I know Mark, and I think I do," looking down and then up as audience laughter is audible.
Ross gestures with his hands.
Ross says, "I'd expect nothing less." Rachel steps away, raising her hands, and says, "I got to figure out what I'm going to wear."
Ross steps toward Monica and says, "High collar and baggy pants say I'm a pro." Audience laughter is audible.
Monica places a hand on the back of the couch.
Rachel pats Monica's arm and says, "Yeah, right. Okay, I'll see you guys later," as she walks away.
Ross points after Rachel and says, "You go get him." He turns to Monica, his brow furrowed, and asks, "What did I do to you? Did I hurt you in some way?"
Monica looks at Ross.
Monica sits on the couch and asks, "Why don't you call him?"
Ross leans over Monica, gesturing with his hands, and says, "Well, thank you very much. Now he's going to prep her. Prep her? As in what you do before you"
Ross mimics a cutting motion with his hands and finishes, "surgically remove the boyfriend." Audience laughter is audible.
Monica stands up.
Monica asks, "Are you?"
Ross points to his chest.
Ross says, "Am I am I out of my mind? Am I losing my senses?" while gesturing with his hands.
Ross touches his chest. Monica watches him.
Ross says, "This dreamy guy is taking my girlfriend out for a"
Monica interrupts, stepping toward him with her hands raised, and says, "you know, this isn't even about you. I mean, this is about Rachel"
Monica keeps her hands raised.
Monica finishes, "and something wonderful happening for her, you know,"
Ross leans forward and says, "even if you're right, what if he wants to sleep with her? Does that mean he gets"
Monica tilts her head.
Monica asks, "I mean, don't you trust her?" Ross replies, "Well, yeah." Monica points at him and says, "Then get over yourself."
Monica continues, "Grow up," and turns to sit on the couch.
Ross looks away, then lowers his gaze. He stands still in front of the couch.
Ross softly says, "You grow." Audience laughter is audible.
Ross glances to the side with his mouth slightly open. He stands with his arms at his sides as audience laughter continues.
The setting changes to a dining room where several people sit around a large table. Joey sits between two women. Chandler sits across from Joey.
Chandler eats from his plate, chewing slowly, while glancing back and forth across the table.
Chandler points at his plate with his fork and says, "This tiramisu is excellent."
Chandler looks up toward the end of the table.
Chandler asks, "Did you make it, Mary Angela?" An older woman at the head of the table replies, "No, I did."
Chandler's eyes widen. He pauses with his mouth slightly open as audience laughter is audible.
Chandler says, "it's. Yummy."
Joey continues eating.
Chandler asks, "So, Mary Angela, do you like it?" Joey takes a bite of food.
The older woman says, "Of course. It's her favorite," gesturing with her fork. Chandler nods.
Chandler forms a small, closed-mouth smile and swallows.
Chandler says, "So,"
Chandler rests his hands on the table.
Chandler asks, "Mary Angela, what's your second favorite?" The older woman raises her hand and says, "More of Grandma's tiramisu. So"
Joey leans forward, speaking with his hands raised, and says, "oh, would you just please"
Joey continues speaking to the older woman, gesturing with both hands. Audience laughter is audible.
Joey says, "give me the recipe, because this is great. It's top notch." The older woman gestures with her hand and says, "The recipe dies with me."
Mary Therese, sitting next to Chandler, touches her lips and says, "So will I"
Mary Therese leans in close to Chandler.
Mary Therese leans closer to Chandler and says, "excuse yourself and go to the"
Chandler leans back, waving his hands quickly, and says, "no, no, I was just squinting. That doesn't mean anything." Mary Therese says, "Just do" as audience laughter begins.
Mary Therese grabs Chandler's shoulder and pushes him slightly. She stands up and walks out of the room.
Mary Therese shouts from off-screen, "it."
Chandler looks around the table and stands up.
Chandler points his thumb over his shoulder and says, "Will you excuse me, I have to"
Chandler leaves the table and walks in the direction Mary Therese went. Joey continues eating, his gaze fixed on his plate. Audience laughter is audible.
In an apartment hallway, Chandler walks forward, followed closely by Mary Therese in a red suit.
Mary Therese pushes Chandler against the wall, placing her hands on the wall on either side of his head. Chandler looks at her.
Mary Therese says, "finally."
Mary Therese continues, "I thought we'd never be alone."
Mary Therese asks, "Can I just tell you something?"
Mary Therese says, "I have not stopped thinking about you since the party." She leans in and kisses Chandler on the lips.
Chandler pulls his head back, gasping, and Mary Therese leans in to kiss him again.
Chandler turns his head away, saying, "Look, I may have jumped the gun here." He pushes her arms aside and ducks under them.

Chandler walks into an adjacent living room, gesturing with his hands. Mary Therese follows him. Chandler stops and turns toward her. A sign reading "God Bless Our Happy Home" hangs on the wall. Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler says, "I just got out of a relationship and I'm not really in a commitment kind of place," gesturing toward his chest. Mary Therese steps toward him, smiling, and says, "Oh, me"
Mary Therese says, "neither. God." She places a hand on his chest and touches his lip with her finger. "Mary Angela was right. You do have the softest lips." Chandler gasps.
Chandler pulls his head back slightly and opens his mouth.
Chandler raises his hands near his chest and asks, "You're not Mary Angela?"
Mary Therese says, "No, I'm Mary Therese." Chandler looks away and says, "This is so bad." Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler turns toward the hallway doorway.
Chandler asks, "If you're not Mary Angela, then who is?" A woman with dark hair in a dark dress standing in the doorway says, "I am."
Chandler stares at the woman in the doorway.
Chandler looks at the woman and says, "Oh, it's so bad."
Chandler quickly turns his head back toward the hallway.
Chandler yells "Joey." He then runs toward the doorway as Joey enters. Chandler waves his hands and says, "No Joey."
Chandler backs away as Joey walks into the room, repeating, "No Joey. No Joey." Joey looks at Chandler and asks, "Whoa, what's going on?"
Several other women with dark hair, wearing red outfits, walk into the room and stand behind Joey. Chandler gestures toward Joey.
Chandler says, "You're it. Now run and hide," pointing at Joey, and runs out of the room. Audience laughter is audible.
Joey turns his head toward Mary Therese.
Mary Therese says, "It's no big deal. Chandler was just kissing me because he thought I was Mary Angela," gesturing with her hand. Joey looks at her.
Joey steps forward, saying, "What? How could you do that? How could you think she was Mary Angela?" gesturing with his hands.
Chandler returns to the room and says, "I wasn't sure which one Mary Angela was."
Chandler steps fully back into the room.
Chandler says, "Look, I'm sorry, okay?" holding his hands out.
Chandler gestures to the women, saying, "I was really drunk, and you guys all look really similar."
A woman in a red top steps forward and says, "I say punch him, Joey." Another woman adds, "Yeah,"
Chandler holds his hand up toward the women.
Chandler says, "Joey. You know what? We should all calm down because your brother's not going to punch me," holding a hand out toward the women.
Joey steps forward, looking at Chandler.
Joey says, "Yeah, well, that is usually what I would do. But I just never thought you'd be on the receiving end of it." He raises a hand. "How could you do this?"
An older woman enters the room carrying a cloth and says, "Hey, where'd everybody?" Joey turns to her and says, "No, no."
Joey places a hand on the older woman's arm and says, "Why don't you go into the den? I think Cops is on," guiding her out of the room. Audience laughter is audible.
Joey closes the door behind the older woman, then turns back to Chandler. Joey's jaw tightens.
Chandler steps toward Joey, gestures with his hand, and says, "Joey. If you want to punch me, go ahead."
Chandler continues, "I deserve it. But I just want you to know that I would never soberly hurt you or your family. You're my best friend," gesturing with his hands.
Chandler places his hands together and says, "and I would never do anything like this ever again." Audience laughter is audible.
The women continue to stand with their arms crossed.
A woman in a red top with her arms crossed says, "So what? I say punch him." The other women start shouting in agreement.
The women continue shouting. Joey turns to them and holds up a hand to quiet them.
Joey gestures with his hand and says, "Nobody punches Chandler."
A woman in red steps forward, saying, "I'll do it." Joey points at her and says, "No, you won't. Look, he knows he did a terrible thing," gesturing toward Chandler.
Joey continues, "And I believe him. He's sorry. But you got one more apology to make, all right? You got to apologize to Mary Angela."
Chandler nods and says, "Okay."
Chandler gestures and says, "Absolutely." Joey says, "All right,"
Joey steps back. Chandler turns to the group of women, his hands clasped in front of him. The women stand with their arms crossed.
Chandler looks from one woman to the next. The women stare back at him without moving. Audience laughter grows louder.
Joey says, "Cookie, now you can punch."
Chandler turns his head toward Joey and asks, "What?"
A punching sound is heard as the scene changes to a lobby with elevator doors. Ross sits on a bench. A man in a suit presses the elevator button.
The elevator doors open, and Rachel exits carrying a single red flower. The man walks into the elevator. Rachel walks toward Ross, smiling.
Rachel asks, "Hey! What are you doing here?" Ross stands up and replies, "Hi. Uh, you know, this building's on my paper route, so..."
Rachel laughs slightly, lightly hits Ross's chest with the flower, and says, "Oh." They lean in and kiss. Rachel says, "Hi."
Ross replies, "Hi. How'd it go?" Rachel says, "Oh, well, the woman I interviewed with was..."
Rachel continues speaking. Her gaze shifts slightly while Ross listens beside her.
In a hallway, Ross and Rachel stand facing each other. Rachel holds a red flower.
Ross says, "So,"
Ross maintains his gaze on Rachel.
Ross says, "listen,"
Ross continues looking at Rachel.
Ross says, "I'm sorry I've been so crazy and jealous."
Ross says, "And it's just I like you a lot"
Rachel looks at Ross.
Rachel nods and says, "I know."
Ross says, "Yeah." Mark approaches from the side and says, "Rachel?"
Rachel turns her head toward Mark.
Rachel says, "Yeah? Hi. Hi, Mark."
Mark says, "Hi. I just talked to Joanna, and she loves you. You got it. You got the job."
Rachel's eyes widen.
Rachel says, "I did?"
Mark nods. Rachel turns to Ross excitedly, then turns back to Mark.
Mark says, "Congratulations," pulling Rachel into a hug.

Rachel squeals. Ross raises his fists and smiles, then his expression drops. Audience cheers are audible. Text reads "EXECUTIVE PRODUCERS KEVIN S. BRIGHT MARTA KAUFFMAN DAVID CRANE". The screen turns black.
The setting changes to an apartment living room where Monica, Chandler, Joey, Ross, and Rachel sit. Phoebe walks in holding a mug.
Monica asks, "So, Phoebe, how was your date?" Phoebe walks further into the room.
Phoebe stops and replies, "Oh, well, you know."
Chandler smiles. Monica says, "Yeah, I do know." Audience laughter is heard.
Phoebe turns her head toward Monica.
Phoebe says, "You were eavesdropping."
Rachel gestures slightly and says, "Eavesdropping? Phoebe's, ceiling tiles were falling down." Audience laughter is audible.
Phoebe places her hand on her chest.
Phoebe says, "Oh, I'm sorry. But I really like this guy."
Phoebe lowers her hand, smiling, and says, "And I think he really happens to like me."
Phoebe stands quietly, looking slightly upward. The others sit silently, watching her.
Ross says, "Maybe he's just jumping on a pogo stick and he really likes it." Audience laughter is audible.
Phoebe continues looking upward with a still expression.
Ross adds, "Maybe the pogo stick likes it too." Audience laughter is heard.
Joey leans forward on the couch.
Joey points forward and says, "All right, that's it. He cannot do this to Phoebe."
Joey stands up, walks toward the door, and says, "This guy's going to get the butt kicking of a lifetime."
Joey turns back to ask, "But is he a big guy?" Ross stands up, gesturing forward, and says, "We'll all go. Come on." Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler, remaining seated, says, "Well, you see. I just got beat up by a girl." Audience laughter is heard.
Chandler stands up.
Chandler says, "They need me." Audience laughter is audible.
Joey opens the door and exits, followed by Ross and Chandler.
Phoebe says, "Thanks, you guys. Thank you."
Joey says from the hallway, "Don't worry."
The door closes behind Chandler.
Monica pats Phoebe's leg and says, "Hope they kick his ass."
Rachel looks at Phoebe.
Rachel says, "Honey, I'm sorry."
Rachel continues, "If it's any consolation he really did sound like he was having more fun with you." Audience laughter is heard.
Phoebe looks upwards, her facial muscles tightening.
Phoebe begins to cry visibly, whining "It's." Audience laughter is audible. Text appears on screen reading "director of photography Mikel Neiers".
Phoebe continues to cry, looking upwards. Additional production credits appear on screen over the characters.
Upbeat instrumental music begins playing. Phoebe keeps crying while Monica and Rachel look at her. More credits appear.
The view changes to the Warner Bros. Television end credits screen over a black background.
The screen remains black.
The black screen continues until the segment ends.
